name: Class Recap — July 9
homework: Review Lesson 5 (verb conjugation — て-form / た-form). Next class (7/16): Parker-san's story, Easy Japanese skit #37 (〜んですが to explain a problem and ask for help politely), verb-conjugation review, and an N5 kanji test — just give it a try for motivation!

summary.h: 〜てくれる — a favor for me
items: て-form + くれる means someone does an action as a favor, kindness, or benefit for the speaker (or someone close).
ジェイさんが看板を描いてくれました = Jay drew the sign for me.
た-form + り、た-form + り + する = 'I do things like A and B' — representative examples, with other activities implied.
踊ったり、ケーキを食べたりしました = We did things like dancing and eating cake.
Contrast with て: 〜たり〜たり lists examples, while 〜て puts actions in strict time order (A, then B).
A modifier — even a whole clause — comes before the noun it describes.
For a long sentence, focus on the end for the main idea; the phrases before a noun are modifying it.
果物がたくさん乗ったアイスクリーム = ice cream topped with lots of fruit (main noun: ice cream).
ボーフムとイエーナに住んでいる友達に会うつもりです = I plan to meet friends who live in Bochum and Jena.
Easy Japanese #37 introduces 〜んですが — politely explain a trouble, then ask for help.
Review Lesson 5 verb conjugation. There will also be an N5 kanji test — just try it for motivation.

groups.name: 〜てくれる — A Favor For Me
groups.notes: て-form + くれる = someone does something as a kindness/benefit for me.

cards.back: someone does something for me (as a favor)
cards.notes: て-form + くれる. Kindness/benefit directed at the speaker or someone close.

cards.front: かんばんをかいてくれました
cards.frontSub: 看板を描いてくれました
cards.notes: ジェイさんが看板を描いてくれました = Jay drew the sign for me.

groups.name: 〜たり〜たりする — Listing Activities
groups.notes: た-form + り、た-form + り + する = do things like A and B (examples; others implied).

cards.back: do things like A and B (examples; others implied)
cards.notes: た-form + り. A non-exhaustive list of activities.

cards.front: おどったり、たべたりしました
cards.frontSub: 踊ったり、食べたりしました
cards.back: We did things like dancing and eating
cards.notes: Random examples — other activities are implied.

cards.back: たり = examples (A, B, …); て = strict sequence (A, then B)
cards.notes: 〜たり〜たり lists things like A and B; 〜て orders them in time.

groups.name: Noun Modification & Long Sentences
groups.notes: Modifiers come before the noun; the main idea is at the end of the sentence.

cards.back: the modifier (even a whole clause) comes before the noun
cards.notes: Focus on the end of a long sentence for the main idea; earlier phrases modify the noun.

cards.front: くだものがたくさんのったアイスクリーム
cards.frontSub: 果物がたくさん乗ったアイスクリーム
cards.back: ice cream topped with lots of fruit
cards.notes: 乗った modifies アイスクリーム — the main noun is 'ice cream'.

cards.front: ボーフムとイエーナにすんでいるともだち
cards.frontSub: ボーフムとイエーナに住んでいる友達
cards.back: friends who live in Bochum and Jena
cards.backSub: Boofumu to Ieena ni sunde iru tomodachi
cards.notes: 住んでいる modifies 友達. …友達に会うつもりです = plan to meet those friends.

groups.name: Prep for 7/16 — 〜んですが
groups.notes: Easy Japanese #37: 〜んですが to explain a problem and ask for help politely.

cards.back: politely explain a situation/trouble, then ask for help
cards.notes: State the background with んですが, then make your request. (Easy Japanese #37)
